about.japanDescription: Japan is globally renowned for the exceptional quality, reliability, and affordability of its used vehicles and auto parts. At Biwako International Trading., LTD. we leverage this reputation as your premier export partner. We specialize in a comprehensive range of vehicles—including cars, SUVs, trucks, and buses—as well as heavy machinery such as excavators, forklifts, and cranes. Beyond complete units, we also offer an extensive inventory of high-quality used auto parts, ranging from engines and suspensions to half-cuts and tires. Our service model covers the entire process: sourcing, dismantling, container loading, and global shipping. By managing all Japanese export procedures and documentation, we simplify the purchasing process for international buyers. To efficiently meet customer demand, we are active members of all major auto auctions in Japan and have established strong business relationships with local automobile dealers.
article.import.body:
Japan is the world leader in quality used vehicles and parts. Importers, auto dealers and wholesalers have long appreciated the advantages of purchasing used parts directly from Japan: low price, high quality, wide range. In this guide we cover the entire process — from choosing a supplier to receiving the cargo at your warehouse.

Step 1 — Choosing a Reliable Supplier in Japan

The first and most important step is to find a licensed exporter in Japan. Pay attention to the following criteria:

  • Dismantling and export license — the supplier must have official Japanese government permits
  • Membership in Japanese auto auctions — this guarantees access to a wide range of vehicles
  • Multilingual support — critical for clear communication about your order
  • Experience with your target market — knowledge of customs and port specifics
  • Real photos and reviews — request photos of previous containers

Biwako International Trading has been operating since 2003, holds all necessary licenses, has multilingual managers and has shipped over 2,500 containers worldwide.

Step 2 — Defining the Parts and Order Volume

Before placing an order, clearly define:

  • Makes and models of vehicles whose parts you need
  • Type of parts: engines, automatic/manual transmissions, body parts, suspension, electrics
  • Supply format: half-cut (front half with engine), nose-cut (front section), individual parts or mixed container
  • Volume: 20-foot or 40-foot container

Not sure what to order? We can help!

Send us your parts list — we will find the best option and calculate the container cost.

Request a Quote

Step 3 — Choosing the Shipping Method

Two main methods are available:

RoRo (Roll-on/Roll-off)

Vehicles drive onto the vessel under their own power. Suitable for complete vehicles. Cheaper than a container, but not suitable for individual parts.

Container Shipping

Parts, half-cuts, nose-cuts, tires — everything is packed into a 20- or 40-foot container. The most popular method for wholesale parts purchases.

Main destination ports: Vladivostok (closest to Japan), as well as ports in other regions. Shipments from Japan depart from Toyama, Yokohama, Kobe ports.

Step 4 — Placing the Order and Payment

After agreeing on the container contents:

  1. The supplier issues a proforma invoice with the parts list and cost
  2. You make payment by bank transfer (T/T)
  3. The supplier purchases vehicles at auctions or from stock
  4. Dismantling, packaging and container loading are performed
  5. You receive the Bill of Lading and export documents

Step 5 — Customs Clearance

When importing parts, you will need to:

  • File a customs declaration through a customs broker
  • Provide: invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin
  • Pay customs duties and applicable taxes
  • Undergo customs inspection (if required)

Tip: Find an experienced customs broker in advance — this saves time and money.

Step 6 — Receiving the Cargo

After customs clearance the container is delivered to your warehouse. Average transit time from Japan to Vladivostok is 5–10 days; to other destinations 3–4 weeks including customs and rail delivery.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the minimum order?

The minimum order is one 20-foot container. We can also arrange a consolidated container for smaller quantities.

Can I order specific makes and models?

Yes. We are members of all major Japanese auto auctions and can source parts to your specification: Toyota, Nissan, Honda, Subaru, Lexus, Mitsubishi and others.

How can I verify the quality of parts?

We provide photos of every part before shipment, auction sheets with the vehicle condition rating, and conduct our own quality control before loading.

Ready to place your first order?

Contact us — we respond in English within 24 hours.

Order a Parts Container

article.polukar.body:
If you are involved in importing Japanese auto parts, you have surely heard the terms "half-cut" and "nose-cut". These are specific vehicle dismantling formats that allow you to get the maximum valuable parts at minimum shipping costs. Let's break it down in detail.

What Is a Half-cut?

A half-cut is the front half of a vehicle, cut approximately at the center of the body (behind the front seats). A half-cut includes:

  • Engine assembly
  • Transmission (automatic or manual)
  • Front suspension
  • Steering system
  • Electrical wiring of the front section
  • Front fenders, hood, bumper
  • Radiator, air conditioner, intercooler
  • Dashboard (depending on dismantling type)
💡 Why it's cost-effective: Instead of buying an engine, transmission and suspension separately, you get everything in one — already removed, inspected and ready to install. The price of a half-cut is significantly lower than the sum of individual parts.

What Is a Nose-cut?

A nose-cut is the front section of a vehicle, cut behind the engine bay. A nose-cut includes:

  • Front bumper
  • Headlights and fog lights
  • Radiator grille
  • Hood
  • Front fenders
  • Cooling radiator
  • Front frame/body section

A nose-cut does not include the engine and transmission — only the body and optical elements of the front section.

Half-cut vs Nose-cut — What's the Difference?

Half-cut

  • Front half of the vehicle
  • Includes engine and transmission
  • More expensive than nose-cut
  • For powertrain replacement
  • Popular in Russia and CIS

Nose-cut

  • Front body section only
  • No engine or transmission
  • Cheaper than half-cut
  • For body repair
  • Popular in Africa and Asia

Advantages of Buying a Half-cut from Japan

  1. Cost savings — a half-cut is cheaper than buying engine + transmission + suspension separately
  2. Compatibility — all parts from the same vehicle, same year of manufacture
  3. Japanese quality — Japanese vehicles are known for low mileage and good condition
  4. Auction sheet — every vehicle has a condition rating from an independent inspector
  5. Wide selection — Toyota, Nissan, Honda, Subaru, Lexus, Mitsubishi and more

Which Makes Are Most Popular for Half-cuts?

  • Toyota Land Cruiser 100/200 — engines 1HD-FTE, 1GR-FE
  • Toyota Camry (XV40, XV50) — engines 2AZ-FE, 2GR-FE
  • Nissan Patrol Y61 — engines TD42, ZD30
  • Honda CR-V (RD1, RE4) — engines B20B, K24A
  • Subaru Forester / Impreza — engines EJ20, EJ25
  • Lexus RX 300/330/350 — engines 1MZ-FE, 2GR-FE
  • Mitsubishi Pajero — engines 6G72, 4M41

How to Order a Half-cut from Japan via Biwako

  1. Contact us and specify the make, model, year and engine type
  2. We will find a suitable vehicle at Japanese auctions
  3. You receive photos and the auction sheet for confirmation
  4. After payment we perform dismantling and packaging
  5. We load into a container and ship

Need a half-cut or nose-cut from Japan?

Specify the make and model — we will source and ship within 2–4 weeks.

Order a Half-cut

article.stoimost.body:
One of the most common questions from importers: "How much does a container of auto parts from Japan cost?" The answer depends on several factors. In this article we break down what makes up the total price, compare 20- and 40-foot containers, and give practical money-saving tips.

What Makes Up the Container Cost

The total price of a parts container from Japan consists of several cost items:

Cost ItemDescription
Parts costAuction vehicle price + dismantling cost
PackagingProtective materials, pallets, fasteners
Container loadingLoading work at the warehouse in Japan
Ocean freightContainer delivery from Japan to destination port
Export documentsInvoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate
Customs dutiesDepends on HS code and cargo value
VATAs applicable in destination country
Broker feesCustoms clearance at destination
Delivery to warehouseFrom port to your warehouse
💡 Important: At Biwako we handle all Japan-side costs — purchasing, dismantling, packaging, loading and export documents. You pay only for the cargo and freight.

20-foot vs 40-foot Container — Which Is Better?

Parameter20 ft (TEU)40 ft (FEU)
Internal volume~33 m³~67 m³
Payloadup to 21 tonsup to 26 tons
Approx. number of engines15–20 pcs35–45 pcs
Ocean freight (Toyama → Vladivostok)lowerhigher, but cheaper per unit
Recommended forFirst order, testRegular wholesale purchases

Conclusion: A 40-foot container is more cost-effective per unit of cargo. For a first order or small business, a 20-foot container is optimal.

Additional Costs Often Overlooked

  • Demurrage — penalty for container idle time at port beyond the free period (usually 5–7 days free)
  • Storage at customs warehouse — if customs clearance is delayed
  • Cargo insurance — recommended, costs 0.3–0.5% of cargo value
  • Certification — may be required for certain types of parts

How to Save on Shipping Parts from Japan

  1. Order 40-foot containers — freight cost per unit of cargo is lower
  2. Plan orders in advance — rush shipments cost more
  3. Choose Vladivostok — the closest Russian port to Japan, minimum freight
  4. Work with a trusted supplier — avoid hidden fees and overpayments
  5. Optimize container contents — mix light and heavy parts to maximize volume utilization

Want to know the exact cost of your container?

Send us your parts list — we will calculate the cost for your request for free.

Get a Cost Estimate

Frequently Asked Questions About Cost

Can I get a cheaper container by working directly with an auction?

No. Japanese auctions only work with licensed dealers. Without a license you cannot participate in bidding. That is why you need a reliable intermediary — a licensed exporter like Biwako.

Is delivery to my warehouse included in the price?

We arrange delivery to the destination port. Delivery from the port to your warehouse is arranged by you or through our partner broker.

article.top10.body:
Over 22 years of operation we have shipped thousands of containers with parts worldwide. Based on real demand from our clients we have compiled a list of the most sought-after Japanese vehicles for wholesale parts purchasing. This ranking will help you form your order correctly.

Ranking by Demand

#1

Toyota Land Cruiser 100 / 200

The absolute demand leader. Parts for Land Cruiser sell instantly — especially engines 1HD-FTE (diesel) and 1GR-FE (petrol), automatic transmissions, front axles and suspension. Half-cuts of LC100 and LC200 are the most popular order format.

Engines 1HD-FTE, 1GR-FEAutomatic TransmissionHalf-cutHigh Demand
#2

Nissan Patrol Y61 / Y62

The second most popular SUV. Diesel engines TD42 and ZD30 are especially in demand, as well as the petrol VQ40DE for Y62. Patrol parts are in stable demand across Russia, Kazakhstan and Central Asia.

Engines TD42, ZD30, VQ40DEHalf-cutRussia, Kazakhstan
#3

Toyota Camry XV40 / XV50 / XV70

The most popular Japanese sedan. Engines 2AZ-FE (2.4) and 2GR-FE (3.5) are in constant demand. Automatic transmissions U660E and U760E are among the most ordered. Camry half-cuts are an excellent option for wholesale purchasing.

Engines 2AZ-FE, 2GR-FEAT U660EHalf-cut
#4

Lexus RX 300 / 330 / 350

Premium crossover with high demand for engines 1MZ-FE and 2GR-FE, automatic transmissions and front suspensions. Lexus RX half-cuts are a popular item among auto dealers.

Engines 1MZ-FE, 2GR-FEHalf-cutPremium Segment
#5

Honda CR-V RD1 / RD4 / RE4

One of the most affordable Japanese crossovers. Engines B20B, K20A, K24A are in stable demand. Honda CR-V half-cuts are a good option for the mid-price segment.

Engines B20B, K24AHalf-cutAffordable Price
#6

Subaru Forester / Outback / Impreza

Subaru with boxer engines EJ20 and EJ25 are very popular. Subaru half-cuts are one of the most ordered formats from Toyama.

Engines EJ20, EJ25Half-cutFar East
#7

Mitsubishi Pajero / Outlander

Engines 6G72 (3.0 V6), 4M41 (diesel) and 4B12 for Outlander are in constant demand. Pajero transfer cases and axles are a popular item for auto dismantlers.

Engines 6G72, 4M41Transfer CaseSUV
#8

Toyota Hilux Surf / 4Runner

Popular body-on-frame SUV. Engines 3RZ-FE, 5VZ-FE and 1KZ-TE are in demand. Half-cuts and individual engines — stable demand.

Engines 3RZ-FE, 5VZ-FEHalf-cutBody-on-frame SUV
#9

Nissan X-Trail T30 / T31

Affordable crossover with high demand for engines QR20DE, QR25DE and MR20DE. X-Trail half-cuts are a popular item for smaller dismantlers.

Engines QR20DE, MR20DEHalf-cutMass Market
#10

Toyota Prius / Alphard / Estima (Hybrids)

Toyota hybrid vehicles are gaining popularity. Hybrid batteries, inverters and engines 2ZR-FXE are in growing demand. A promising direction for wholesale purchasing.

Hybrid BatteriesInvertersGrowing Demand

Need parts for any of these makes?

We work with all Japanese auctions and can source parts to your specification. We respond within 24 hours.

Request Parts Availability

Tips for Forming a Wholesale Order

  • Mix makes — a container with parts for 3–4 different makes sells faster
  • Focus on popular engines — they sell fastest
  • Add automatic transmissions — gearboxes are in constant short supply
  • Don't forget tires — a container of Japanese tires with rims gives good margin
  • Request auction sheets — this confirms condition and helps with sales

testimonial.name: Sergei
testimonial.quote: “I recently received a container of used car parts from Biwako International Trading Japan, and I am very satisfied with their service. The parts were exactly as described, well packed, and delivered safely. The team handled everything professionally, from sourcing the parts to loading the container and managing the export documents. Communication was clear throughout the whole process. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for reliable suppliers of used auto parts from Japan.
